output:
**Yes.** The chance of black hair is larger when observing college degree or higher.

**Computation:**

- P(black hair | college+) = P(college+ ∧ black hair) / P(college+) = 18% / 45% = **40.0%**
- P(black hair | high school−) = P(high school− ∧ black hair) / P(high school−) = 17% / 55% ≈ **30.9%**

40.0% > 30.9%, so observing college degree or higher is associated with a higher probability of black hair.

Since the causal structure is a simple chain (Education → Skill → Black hair) with no confounders or backdoor paths, the conditional probabilities here also reflect the causal direction: higher education increases skill, which in turn increases the probability of black hair.
